why: Dexter is the only current source that simultaneously owns upstream opportunity capture, pre-attempt timing, low-slippage entry, realized exit capture, and stale-risk avoidance.
Dexter remains the broader operational objective winner even after the strategy split is made explicit.
A default execution backbone should inherit the source with active lifecycle control rather than the source whose observed closes remain timeout-shaped.
default_execution_anchor.bound: Choosing Dexter as default does not erase the Mew-X containment edge; it bounds that edge to selective use.

selection_conditions: The strategy objective explicitly prioritizes drawdown containment.
Local-peak retention matters more than realized exit capture.
Fast post-attempt fill is desirable even with worse observed slippage.
Timeout-shaped lifecycle behavior is explicitly acceptable.
The deployment remains a separate execution template, sleeve, or route rather than an in-run fusion layer.
not_supported_for: Mew-X as the default primary engine.
Mew-X as an automatic failover inside a Dexter-managed position.
Mew-X as proof of upstream candidate-quality parity.
Mew-X as justification for threshold or trust-logic transplant into Dexter.
selective_mewx_option.bound: The Mew-X selective lane is only source-faithful when timeout-shaped lifecycle behavior is an accepted trade for containment-first objectives.

supported: Pre-run route selection by explicit objective profile.
A Dexter default lane for opportunity capture, low-slippage entry, realized exit capture, and lifecycle control.
A separate Mew-X containment lane for drawdown containment, fast post-attempt fill, and local-peak retention.
Portfolio split across independent sleeves where the objective charter is fixed before execution begins.
objective_conditioned_routing.default_routing_rule: If objective weights are mixed or unspecified, route to Dexter by default.
not_supported: Mid-trade handoff from Dexter to Mew-X or from Mew-X to Dexter.
A fused single-engine design that mixes both source logics inside one live position lifecycle.
Claims that a blended engine is already proven superior to either standalone source.
Widening replay parity into a full path-equivalence or retention-equivalence proof.

execution_assumptions_fixed_now: Dexter should remain the default execution anchor whenever objective weights are mixed or unspecified.
Mew-X should remain a containment-first selective option rather than a general fallback.
Hybrid use should stay ex ante and objective-conditioned, not in-run and compositional.
No new evidence is required to plan bounded execution at this level.
No validator, Dexter, or Mew-X source logic changes are justified by this planning task.
The replay claim stays bounded to realized-return fidelity and does not block implementation planning.
Path-sensitive retention scope is still optional unless later design work tries to claim stronger retention equivalence than current evidence supports.
execution_questions_not_fixed_now: The exact config surface that declares an objective profile and routes into a Dexter or Mew-X sleeve.
The portfolio budget or capital split between Dexter-default and Mew-X-selective sleeves.
The monitoring and kill-switch rules needed around timeout-shaped Mew-X lifecycle behavior.
The exact abstraction boundary between a planner/router layer and source-specific executors.
Whether any future hybrid implementation would outperform both standalone sources after build-out.
non_goals: Do not collect new live or replay evidence.
Do not change Dexter, Mew-X, or validator rules.
Do not claim in-run source fusion is now validated.
Do not transplant thresholds, trust logic, or semantic assumptions across frozen sources.
Do not widen replay fidelity into full path equivalence.
Do not move to funded live trading.

why: The execution surface is already bounded enough to decompose work into implementation-scoped decisions without another evidence task.
The critical boundary is now fixed: Dexter default, Mew-X selective containment option, objective-conditioned routing, and explicit non-goals.
A separate evidence lane is not required first because the current task already fixes the execution anchor and routing bounds.
Concrete design details can be handled inside implementation planning rather than as another comparison-derived research step.
No current execution recommendation depends on a stronger path-equivalence claim.
This remains optional unless later design work tries to claim stronger retention equivalence than current evidence supports.

residual_and_caveats.replay_scope: Current zero-gap evidence is scoped to close-summary and position_closed realized-return fidelity rather than full execution-path equivalence.

dexter_metric_scope_gap.why_it_matters: A stronger claim about path-sensitive retention still requires a separate lane.

residual_and_caveats.candidate_surface_gap: Mew-X candidate_precision_proxy remains unavailable because the frozen export surface does not expose a comparable creator_candidate path.
residual_and_caveats.execution_plan_boundary: Dexter is the default execution anchor, but Mew-X remains the bounded selective option only for containment-first objectives and not for source fusion.

why: The fixed comparison and strategy layers are now explicit enough to choose a default execution anchor and a bounded selective option.
Dexter remains the default execution backbone for the broader operational objective set.
Mew-X still has a real but bounded role for containment-first sleeves without upgrading into a fused or fallback default.
That is sufficient to move directly into implementation planning without reopening comparison work.
